title: Profit for the biggest U.S. oil companies declined in 1st quarter – but only on paper
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: Profit for the two largest U.S. oil companies fell in the first quarter due to financial hedges backfiring after geopolitical tensions. Exxon's earnings dropped
excerpt: Profit for the two largest U.S. oil companies fell in the first quarter due to financial hedges backfiring after geopolitical tensions. Exxon's earnings dropped to $4.18 billion, while Chevron also saw a decline. Despite the drop, actual revenue likely remained strong due to higher crude and gasolin

content.introduction: Profit for the two largest U.S. oil companies fell in the first quarter due to financial hedges backfiring after geopolitical tensions. Exxon's earnings dropped to $4.18 billion, while Chevron also saw a decline. Despite the drop, actual revenue likely remained strong due to higher crude and gasoline prices.
body: Exxon earned $4.18 billion, or $1 per share, for the period ended March 31. A year earlier it earned $7.7 billion, or $1.76 per share. The
The Morning Call (@mcall). 136 views. Profit for the biggest US oil companies declined in the first quarter, but only on paper.
Exxon and Chevron are among the big drillers reporting earnings this week. On Tuesday BP said that its first-quarter profit more than doubled.
